home.improvementsTitle: What V3 brings in practical terms

improvements.title: Full internationalization
improvements.desc: FR/EN interface, automatic language detection, and locally persisted preference.

improvements.title: Editorial-grade motion
improvements.desc: Hero parallax, scroll reveals, page transitions, and staggered entrances that create rhythm.

improvements.title: Premium micro-interactions
improvements.desc: Button ripple effects, elevated card hover states, image zoom, and animated nav indicator.

improvements.title: More credible reading flow
improvements.desc: Stronger hierarchy, clearer labels, and more coherent components throughout.

items: Stronger hierarchy with a real visual entry point
Primary and secondary CTAs are better prioritized
More breathing room through spacing and motion
A memorable interface without hurting readability

pillars.title: Native bilingual setup
pillars.desc: A coherent FR/EN foundation from interface copy to article content.

pillars.title: Purposeful motion design
pillars.desc: Subtle animation that supports storytelling instead of distracting from it.

pillars.title: Details that matter
pillars.desc: Small moments of feedback make the product feel clearer, sharper, and more premium.

blog.title: All articles
blog.description: A bilingual collection designed to make exploration smoother and more enjoyable.

about.title: Why this version goes further
about.description: This redesign goes beyond a visual facelift: it structures attention better, makes reading more immersive, and opens the door to a truly international experience.
about.body1: V2 already provided a solid base. V3 adds a smarter interface layer: bilingual content, persisted language preference, reactive components, and transitions that give the blog more presence.
about.body2: The goal is not to pile on effects for style alone. It is to increase perceived quality, help readers orient themselves faster, and make the reading journey more engaging without compromising performance or accessibility.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Reach out if you want to talk about tech, product, automation, or collaboration.
contact.cardBody: I'm always open to discussing an ambitious project, a technical topic, or a thoughtful product improvement.
